step_rationale: Haptoglobin is an acute-phase marker and core MI gene; binds hemoglobin in oxidative stress.
mechanism: Haptoglobin is an acute-phase diagnostic biomarker for myocardial infarction, responding to hemolysis and vascular inflammation in atherothrombotic events.

references.title: Upregulated haptoglobin in classical monocytes serves as a diagnostic and immunological biomarker in myocardial infarction: a cross-sectional multi-omics study.
Novel Protein-Based Biomarkers of Out-of-hospital Sudden Cardiac Death After Myocardial Infarction.
Identification of core genes shared by ischemic stroke and myocardial infarction using an integrated approach.
Erythrocyte-heme proteins and STEMI: implications in prognosis.
Hp: an inflammatory indicator in cardiovascular disease.
Proteomic analysis of plasma samples from patients with acute myocardial infarction identifies haptoglobin as a potential prognostic biomarker.
Predicting left ventricular remodeling after a first myocardial infarction by plasma proteome analysis.
